title: What Visuals Show About Recent Damage to U.S. Military Sites in the Middle East
summary: Video footage and satellite images reveal new details about Iranian strikes on U.S. targets during the latest flare-up in fighting.

title: U.S. Military Presence in Jordan Makes It Iranian Target
summary: Relying heavily on U.S. aid and protection, Jordan is unlikely to restrict the American military presence there, despite escalating Iranian airstrikes.

title: U.S. Employers Forced to Fire Thousands of Immigrants, and a Showdown over Peptides
summary: Plus, America says no thanks to salad.

title: U.S.-Saudi Deal Prompts Fears of a Global Nuclear Arms Race
summary: In Europe, Asia and the Middle East, countries seek nuclear capabilities to hedge against aggressive neighbors and a receding Washington, potentially creating a more freewheeling era.

title: Oil-Rich Saudi Arabia Isn’t Short on Energy. Here’s Why It’s Going Nuclear.
summary: The world’s largest exporter of crude oil stands to benefit from diversifying its energy sources — and freeing up more oil to sell.

title: Nuclear Energy Revival Puts Westinghouse in Prime Position
summary: The company, which filed for bankruptcy protection in 2017, stands to benefit from growing support for nuclear power and President Trump’s deal with Saudi Arabia.

title: Google Hit With $1 Billion Fine By EU Over Search Engine Practices
summary: At a tense moment for trans-Atlantic trade, the European Union accused Google of anti-competitive business practices.

title: Google Sets the Pace on Hefty A.I. Spending
summary: The technology giant again raised its projections for investing in artificial intelligence. Many on Wall Street still worry about the eventual payoff.

title: Clarity Act Mired In Debate Over Whether to Bar President From Selling Crypto
summary: Democrats and Republicans are haggling about the Clarity Act, a major bill pushed by the crypto industry, as it moves closer to a Senate vote.

title: A Peptide Showdown: F.D.A. Scientists May Clash With R.F.K.’s Agenda
summary: Agency scientists are advising against allowing widespread use of several peptide injections. Robert F. Kennedy Jr., the health secretary, has said people should be free to experiment.

title: The Devotion of Willow Smith
summary: The daughter of a Hollywood power couple, she has gone above and beyond to prove herself. Virtuosity and spiritual aspirations fill her latest, “The Thread.”

title: Mamdani Knew He Couldn’t Arrest Netanyahu. But He Could Denounce Him.
summary: In a video message, the mayor of New York City said he could not legally arrest Israel’s prime minister but reinforced his position as a voice of the international left.

summary: President Volodymyr Zelensky of Ukraine has pivoted from unpopular moves before, but a recent decision about military leadership and strategy could weaken his position.

title: A New Target for Ukraine’s Long-Range Attacks: Russia’s Version of Amazon
summary: Kyiv is escalating its air war by striking commercial warehouses, trying to bring the conflict closer to home for ordinary Russians.

title: ‘We Will All Die From Ebola Here’: Virus Stalks Crowded Camps in Congo
summary: There are a million people displaced by violence in the epicenter. They live in dangerous conditions and are fighting the virus with very little clean water.

title: How to Eat a 2,000-Pound Fish? These Orcas Choose to Pulverize It.
summary: Marine biologists in Mexico captured video of a strange maneuver that killer whales used to shred their meal.

title: Diesel Crisis Puts Poor Farmers Around the World at Risk
summary: The closure of the Strait of Hormuz has limited the shipment of diesel, depriving farmers of fuel for irrigation pumps and threatening the food supply.

title: How to Use ChatGPT and Gemini Prompts to Find Out What They Know About You
summary: It can be unsettling what Gemini and ChatGPT have figured out about you and how easily your privacy can be punctured. Here’s how to find out.

title: Japan’s Leader Invokes Her Hero, Shinzo Abe, to Drive Nationalist Agenda
summary: Prime Minister Sanae Takaichi is leveraging her ties to Mr. Abe, the slain Japanese leader, as she pushes to reshape defense and the economy.

title: A Russian Spy, Suddenly Cast Into the Spotlight, Flees Japan
summary: The military intelligence officer left the country after The Times reported that he was using the Aeroflot airline office in Tokyo to conduct espionage.

title: Gen Z Fell Out of Love With Work. I Don’t Blame Them.
summary: For Gen Z college graduates, the way millennials lived out their ambitions no longer makes sense.

title: Iran, China, Canada? A Defense of Trump’s Grand Strategy
summary: A Pentagon official argues that “people can no longer take the United States for granted.”

title: Photographic Evidence
summary: We look at how The Times uses photographs and video to better understand what’s happening in the war with Iran.

title: Late Night Rags on Trump and His Cabinet Joining TikTok
summary: “Right now TikTok is, like, ‘Oh, no, are we the new Facebook?’” Jimmy Fallon said on “The Tonight Show.”

title: Amid Cyclospora Outbreak Tied to Lettuce, America Skips Salads
summary: Foot traffic to chains such as Chopt and Chipotle fell in recent weeks after a recall from Taylor Farms and changing messages from the F.D.A.
